name: quantum-harness
description: Drive the verifiable quantum-design bench from inside Claude: list problems, read a BRIEF, re-verify a proof bundle through the real numpy judge, and mint a public run repo.
long_description: A dependency-free MCP connector for QuantumMytheme/quantum-harness. It exposes the harness in the Claude Desktop app so a run never has to leave the chat: pick an open quantum-design problem, read its conceptual BRIEF, design a proof bundle, and re-derive it through the real four-gate numpy judge (structure → reproducibility → performance → anti-overfit) — the exit code is the result, not a claim. mint_run creates a fresh public run repository from the template, and commit_run writes the bundle straight to it via the GitHub Contents API — no Docker, no git, no second connector.

tools.name: list_problems
tools.description: List the open problems the judge can grade.

tools.name: get_brief
tools.description: Read a problem's BRIEF — the target stated conceptually.

tools.name: get_kickoff
tools.description: Read KICKOFF.md — the one-message run contract.

tools.name: verify_bundle
tools.description: Re-derive a proof bundle through the real four-gate numpy judge.

tools.name: mint_run
tools.description: Create a fresh public run repository from the template.

tools.name: commit_run
tools.description: Commit a proof bundle to a run repo via the GitHub Contents API (no Docker, no git); refuses a REJECT by default.

tools.name: mint_recipe
tools.description: Mint a FULL-STACK design run repo: create it from the template and write the RECIPE.json (hardware + software) into it; reports whether the hardware target is referee-pinned.

github_token.title: GitHub token
github_token.description: GitHub token used by mint_run (create your run repo) and commit_run (write the bundle to it). Least privilege: a fine-grained PAT with Administration:write + Contents:write and a short expiry; classic public_repo also works. Leave blank to skip minting/committing — every other tool works without it.
